proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `treatment_enzalutamide=1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `treatment_enzalutamide=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by treatment_enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.361377 with treatment_enzalutamide=1 (7255/20076) versus 0.158936 with treatment_enzalutamide=0 (4756/29924), a risk difference of +0.202441; p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `mcrpc=1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `mcrpc=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by mcrpc.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.153524 with mcrpc=1 versus 0.346019 with mcrpc=0, a risk difference of -0.192495; p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_enzalutamide` and `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `mcrpc=1` than in patients with `mcrpc=0`.

analyses.code: Four-cell risk-difference interaction: RD(enzalutamide | mcrpc=1) - RD(enzalutamide | mcrpc=0).
analyses.result_summary: The enzalutamide risk difference was +0.440293 in mcrpc=0 but only +0.007655 in mcrpc=1; interaction difference-in-differences was -0.432638; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test among mcrpc=1 patients.
analyses.result_summary: Within mcrpc=1 patients, response was 0.158105 on enzalutamide versus 0.150450 off enzalutamide, risk difference +0.007655; p=0.085650.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `ar_v7_positive=1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `ar_v7_positive=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by ar_v7_positive.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.159892 with ar_v7_positive=1 versus 0.260397 with ar_v7_positive=0, a risk difference of -0.100505; p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_enzalutamide` and `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `ar_v7_positive=1` than in patients with `ar_v7_positive=0`.

analyses.code: Four-cell risk-difference interaction: RD(enzalutamide | ar_v7_positive=1) - RD(enzalutamide | ar_v7_positive=0).
analyses.result_summary: The enzalutamide risk difference was +0.250989 in ar_v7_positive=0 but +0.008462 in ar_v7_positive=1; interaction difference-in-differences was -0.242527; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test among ar_v7_positive=1 patients.
analyses.result_summary: Within ar_v7_positive=1 patients, response was 0.164966 on enzalutamide versus 0.156504 off enzalutamide, risk difference +0.008462; p=0.259030.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `brca2_mutation=1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `brca2_mutation=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by brca2_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.149720 with brca2_mutation=1 versus 0.250267 with brca2_mutation=0, a risk difference of -0.100547; p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_enzalutamide` and `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `brca2_mutation=1` than in patients with `brca2_mutation=0`.

analyses.code: Four-cell risk-difference interaction: RD(enzalutamide | brca2_mutation=1) - RD(enzalutamide | brca2_mutation=0).
analyses.result_summary: The enzalutamide risk difference was +0.224572 in brca2_mutation=0 but +0.006320 in brca2_mutation=1; interaction difference-in-differences was -0.218252; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test among brca2_mutation=1 patients.
analyses.result_summary: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, response was 0.153468 on enzalutamide versus 0.147148 off enzalutamide, risk difference +0.006320; p=0.539830.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `mcrpc=0`, `ar_v7_positive=0`, and `brca2_mutation=0`, `treatment_enzalutamide=1` is associated with a much higher `objective_response` rate than `treatment_enzalutamide=0`.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test restricted to mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0, brca2_mutation=0.
analyses.result_summary: In mcrpc=0/ar_v7_positive=0/brca2_mutation=0 patients, response was 0.780012 with enzalutamide versus 0.173274 without enzalutamide, a risk difference of +0.606738; p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `treatment_abiraterone=1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `treatment_abiraterone=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by treatment_abiraterone.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.238076 with abiraterone versus 0.241138 without abiraterone, a risk difference of -0.003062; p=0.462000. This does not support a higher response rate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `treatment_docetaxel=1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `treatment_docetaxel=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by treatment_docetaxel.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.239876 with docetaxel versus 0.240370 without docetaxel, a risk difference of -0.000494; p=0.905370. This does not support a higher response rate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `brca2_mutation=1`, `treatment_olaparib=1` is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `treatment_olaparib=0`.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test restricted to brca2_mutation=1.
analyses.result_summary: Among brca2_mutation=1 patients, response was 0.120983 with olaparib versus 0.153123 without olaparib, a risk difference of -0.032140; p=0.034094. The statistically significant direction was opposite the hypothesis.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model with treatment_olaparib, brca2_mutation, olaparib_x_brca2, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, psa_q75, and treatment_enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted olaparib_x_brca2 coefficient was -0.034709 on the response probability scale; p=0.051587, again not supporting a positive BRCA2-specific olaparib association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `psma_high=1`, `treatment_lu177_psma=1` is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `treatment_lu177_psma=0`.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test restricted to psma_high=1.
analyses.result_summary: Among psma_high=1 patients, response was 0.237628 with Lu177-PSMA versus 0.238774 without it, risk difference -0.001146; p=0.868010.

analyses.code: Four-cell risk-difference interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and psma_high.
analyses.result_summary: The treatment_lu177_psma by psma_high interaction difference was -0.010007; p=0.361840, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `msi_high=1`, `treatment_pembrolizumab=1` is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `treatment_pembrolizumab=0`.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test restricted to msi_high=1.
analyses.result_summary: Among msi_high=1 patients, response was 0.177215 with pembrolizumab versus 0.175983 without pembrolizumab, risk difference +0.001232; p=0.977720.

analyses.code: Four-cell risk-difference interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high.
analyses.result_summary: The treatment_pembrolizumab by msi_high interaction difference was +0.002767; p=0.951020.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `her2_amplification=1`, `treatment_pembrolizumab=1` is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `treatment_pembrolizumab=0`.

analyses.code: Stratified two-proportion z-test restricted to her2_amplification=1.
analyses.result_summary: Among her2_amplification=1 patients, response was 0.375000 with pembrolizumab (30/80) versus 0.214535 without pembrolizumab, risk difference +0.160465; p=0.003652.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model including pembrolizumab, msi_high, pembro_x_msi, her2_amplification, pembro_x_her2, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted pembro_x_her2 interaction coefficient was +0.130957; p=0.013768. This is exploratory because the treated HER2-amplified cell had only 80 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `msi_high=1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `msi_high=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by msi_high.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.176047 with msi_high=1 versus 0.242243 with msi_high=0, a risk difference of -0.066196; p=2.708e-11.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model including pembrolizumab/HER2 terms, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted msi_high coefficient was -0.061422; p=1.5163e-8.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `psma_high=1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `psma_high=0`.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response by psma_high.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.238602 with psma_high=1 versus 0.242639 with psma_high=0, a risk difference of -0.004037; p=0.301020. This refutes a positive main association.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model including treatment_lu177_psma, psma_high, lu177_x_psma, visceral_mets, lu177_x_visceral, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted psma_high coefficient was -0.003260; p=0.411950.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `ecog_ps=2` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `ecog_ps` of 0 or 1.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing ecog_ps=2 to ecog_ps in {0,1}.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.181525 in ecog_ps=2 versus 0.250476 in ecog_ps 0-1, risk difference -0.068951; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model with clinical risk variables, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ecog2 coefficient was -0.067016; p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `psa_ng_ml` in the top quartile (`psa_ng_ml>=41.28`) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients below that threshold.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing psa_ng_ml>=41.28 to lower PSA.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.152548 for psa_ng_ml>=41.28 versus 0.269447 below 41.28, risk difference -0.116899; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model with clinical risk variables, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted psa_q75 coefficient was -0.021099; p=1.2137e-6, indicating attenuation but persistent negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `weight_loss_pct_6mo` in the top quartile (`weight_loss_pct_6mo>=6.4`) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients below that threshold.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing weight_loss_pct_6mo>=6.4 to lower weight loss.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.211355 for weight_loss_pct_6mo>=6.4 versus 0.249920 below 6.4, risk difference -0.038565; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model with clinical risk variables, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted weight_loss_q75 coefficient was -0.039408; p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `crp_mg_l` in the top quartile (`crp_mg_l>=6.9825`) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients below that threshold.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing crp_mg_l>=6.9825 to lower CRP.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.220960 for crp_mg_l>=6.9825 versus 0.246640 below 6.9825, risk difference -0.025680; p=2.9599e-9.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model with clinical risk variables, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted crp_q75 coefficient was -0.026846; p=3.8844e-11.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `albumin_g_dl` in the bottom quartile (`albumin_g_dl<=3.5`) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients above that threshold.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing albumin_g_dl<=3.5 to higher albumin.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.230844 for albumin_g_dl<=3.5 versus 0.244398 above 3.5, risk difference -0.013554; p=0.00096331.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model with clinical risk variables, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted albumin_low_q25 coefficient was -0.014963; p=9.6882e-5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with high-grade `gleason_score` values of 9 or 10 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `gleason_score` values from 6 through 8.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing gleason_score>=9 to gleason_score<=8.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.241864 for gleason_score 9-10 versus 0.239814 for gleason_score 6-8, risk difference +0.002049; p=0.669690. This does not support lower response in high Gleason score.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_lu177_psma` and `objective_response` is lower in patients with `visceral_mets=1` than in patients with `visceral_mets=0`.

analyses.code: Four-cell risk-difference interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and visceral_mets.
analyses.result_summary: The Lu177-PSMA risk difference was +0.009868 in visceral_mets=0 but -0.024328 in visceral_mets=1; interaction difference-in-differences was -0.034195; p=0.0086517.

analyses.code: Adjusted HC1 linear probability model including treatment_lu177_psma, psma_high, lu177_x_psma, visceral_mets, lu177_x_visceral, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and enzalutamide.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted lu177_x_visceral coefficient was -0.030829; p=0.012094.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with four or more adverse factors among `mcrpc`, `psa_ng_ml>=41.28`, `ecog_ps=2`, `weight_loss_pct_6mo>=6.4`, `crp_mg_l>=6.9825`, `albumin_g_dl<=3.5`, `ar_v7_positive=1`, and `brca2_mutation=1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with fewer than four adverse factors.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing adverse factor count >=4 versus <4.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.125983 in patients with >=4 adverse factors versus 0.257245 with <4 factors, risk difference -0.131262; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Descriptive trend by adverse factor count.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates declined from 0.456288 with 0 adverse factors to 0.319205 with 1, 0.214513 with 2, 0.151417 with 3, 0.137770 with 4, 0.092776 with 5, and 0.063559 with 6 factors; counts above 6 were very sparse. The dichotomized >=4 vs <4 adverse-factor comparison in the same iteration was statistically significant at p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an adjusted linear probability model for `objective_response`, `treatment_enzalutamide` remains positively associated with response in the reference subgroup, while the `treatment_enzalutamide` interactions with `mcrpc`, `ar_v7_positive`, and `brca2_mutation` remain negative.

analyses.code: HC1 linear probability model: objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide + mcrpc + treatment_enzalutamide:mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + treatment_enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + treatment_enzalutamide:brca2_mutation + psa_q75 + ecog2 + weight_loss_q75 + crp_q75 + albumin_low_q25.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted reference-subgroup treatment_enzalutamide coefficient was +0.509340 (p<1e-300), while interactions were negative: enzal_x_mcrpc -0.431074 (p<1e-300), enzal_x_arv7 -0.240970 (p<1e-300), and enzal_x_brca2 -0.216710 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Same HC1 adjusted model; interaction coefficient for treatment_enzalutamide:mcrpc.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_enzalutamide:mcrpc interaction coefficient was -0.431074; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Same HC1 adjusted model; interaction coefficient for treatment_enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive interaction coefficient was -0.240970; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Same HC1 adjusted model; interaction coefficient for treatment_enzalutamide:brca2_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_enzalutamide:brca2_mutation interaction coefficient was -0.216710; p<1e-300.
